description:
Document concerning the sale of shares in sugar refinery. At the top appears the name Saʿadya b. Shelomo b. Yefet ha-Levi (all given titles such as peʾer and hod ha-leviyim). There follow calculations regarding the proceeds from the sale of a sugar refinery (maṭbakh). There follows a block of text describing the maṭbakh which used to be known by the name of "the sons of al-Lebdī" (awlād al-Lebdī), mentioning Dīwān al-Aḥbās in Fustat (the government bureau in charge of waqf properties), and ending with the line, "its fame makes its description unnecessary." Related to T-S Ar.49.13 (PGPID 33843). Merits further examination.

There follow several lines of Arabic script in red ink, probably pen trials, including the word "waṣal" several times, then "fī yawm al...," then "Cancer rose." There are also two lines in both Arabic and Judaeo-Arabic on verso.
description_he: מסמך משפטי בArabic, Judaeo-Arabic. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
